text: One person that made me feel the magic of coding. And I remember him going like 'Ajke ekta bombastic problem solve korbo'

In terms of teaching he's the GOAT without any doubt. But apart from the teaching part no one can match the gem of a human being sir is. Sir is such a kind soul that when his students didn't do well in an exam sir blames himself and tell 'maybe I did lack in teaching you guys'. But in reality it was fault of the students because we didn't study properly.
KING! GOAT
GOAT PURAI

The best human, mentor, teacher you will ever find in your BracU journey. The man with zero hater! Always ready to help you. You are unlucky if you don't get the chance to meet him & attend his class.
GOAT of all GOATS
Best CSE110 faculty
He is one of the best faculty in BRACU I have ever seen, he is a good human being also, he gives many instruction, which as a freshers I need most.
If you follow his lecture notes closely and ask questions upfront, his insights are genuinely deep. Very approachable on Discord — always responsive.
Moves too fast and relies heavily on slides without simplifying complex logic. Great personality outside class, but classroom delivery needs work.
Polarizing faculty. Brilliant person and super available, but teaching clarity is inconsistent. Works best if you are a self-learner.
Quizzes feel unnecessarily difficult and pacing is chaotic. Outside class he's great — Discord availability is 10/10. High variance experience.
Use his consultation hours and stay on top of the material and it becomes manageable. Not for passive learners at all.
Recent semesters have dropped significantly. Students from 230 are saying run — I would echo that. His past reputation does not reflect the current experience.
One of the hardest courses made harder by fast-paced teaching. Marking is inconsistent — can be lenient one exam, CG crusher the next.
Course notes are dense but valuable. His Discord support is the only reason I passed. Would not take again but respect the effort he puts in outside class.
Generally a good experience. Quizzes are easy but finals are a different beast. Build a good rapport early and she is very supportive.
Teaching is monotonous and speech-heavy. Clear favouritism in marking — if she likes you, grade bumps happen. If not, expect a tough time.
She was genuinely great with me — supportive, fair marks, and always willing to help in labs. Course load is very manageable.
High-risk high-reward. Her class is a split experience — some students rave, some walk away feeling targeted. Stay on her good side.
Labs are fine and she genuinely helps out during sessions. Lectures can drag but you cover what you need. Watch for inconsistency in marking.
Described as angel by some and CG destroyer by others — both are accurate depending on which side you fall on. Average teaching, politics-heavy marking.
Reads directly from slides and has a weak grasp of advanced Java. Her notes and past papers are excellent but class attendance barely helps.
Mocked a student for asking a question during consultation. Behavior is unacceptable. Below-average teaching. The only saving grace is the past paper collection.
Once praised as goated and pookie, but the last two semesters have been a completely different person. Volatile, mocking, and unpredictable. Proceed with extreme caution.
Run fly or die — the warning is real. Her resources are the best in the department for CSE220 but the emotional toll of her behavior is not worth it for most students.
For a first programming course, clarity is everything and she does not deliver it. Uses ChatGPT-generated code examples sometimes which confuses more than it helps.
Behavior is the biggest concern. Known to be dismissive of female students and harsh to anyone she perceives as not trying. Not a comfortable learning environment.
